Within the first 2 minutes 119 (79.3%) applicants were able to insert the LMAS and provide tidal volumes greater than 150 ml (estimated dead space). Time to insertion and first ventilation was 83.2 ± 29 s. No significant difference related to previous BLS training (P = 0.85), technical education (P = 0.07) or gender could be demonstrated (P = 0.25).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that laypersons who receive similar instruction and practice will demonstrate comparable success rates in inserting an LMAS and providing adequate ventilation during simulated emergency scenarios as compared to a controlled laboratory setting.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [Key findings include] The majority of laypersons (96.7%) were able to correctly insert a Laryngeal Mask Supreme® (LMAS) into a manikin within two minutes when provided with an instruction manual consisting of four diagrams. Additionally, 79.3% of participants provided tidal volumes greater than 150 ml within the first two minutes, indicating successful ventilation.

GAP: [The knowledge gap identified is] While the study shows that laypersons can successfully insert an LMAS and provide adequate ventilation with guidance, it does not explore how these skills translate to real-world scenarios where the environment might be more chaotic and stressful.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that laypersons who receive similar instruction and practice will demonstrate comparable success rates in inserting an LMAS and providing adequate ventilation during simulated emergency scenarios as compared to a controlled laboratory setting.

abstract: The aim of this work is to assess a previously described slit-lamp biomicroscopy-based method (SLBM) for measuring pupil diameter and compare it to Colvard infrared pupillometry (CIP). Two examiners performed three repeated measurements with each instrument in 40 healthy eyes. We determined the agreement of SLBM and CIP, intraobserver and interobserver repeatabilities, and interobserver concordance (kappa) and SLBM ability for detecting pupil sizes over 6.0 mm. The mean (±standard deviation [SD]) pupil diameter was 5.81 ± 0.70 mm with SLBM and 6.26 ± 0.68 mm with CIP (p = 0.01) averaging both examiner's results. Mean differences between the SLBM and CIP were -0.60 mm and -0.30 mm for each examiner using the average of the three readings (p = 0.02), and they were very similar using the first reading. Intraobserver reproducibility: the width of the 95% LoA ranged from 1.79 to 2.30 mm. The ICCs were 0.97 and 0.92 for SLBM, and 0.96 and 0.90 for CIP. Interobserver reproducibility: the width of the LoA ranged from 1.82 to 2.09 mm. Kappa statistics were 0.39 and 0.49 for the first and mean SLBM readings, respectively, and 0.45 for both the first and mean CIP readings. Sensitivity and specificity of SLBM for detection of pupils larger than 6 mm ranged from 55.56% to 73.68% and from 76.19% to 95.45%, respectively. The best trade-off between sensitivity and specificity ranged from 5.4 mm to 6.2 mm.
hypothesis: While the study provides a comparison of SLBM and CIP in terms of agreement, intraobserver and interobserver repeatability, and sensitivity for detecting larger pupils, it does not explore the impact of various environmental factors (such as ambient lighting
raw_output:
FINDINGS: 
1. The mean pupil diameter measured by SLBM and CIP differed significantly (p = 0.01).
2. There were small but statistically significant mean differences between the two methods (-0.60 mm and -0.30 mm).
3. Both SLBM and CIP showed high intraobserver repeatability with ICCs ranging from 0.97 to 0.92.
4. Interobserver repeatability was also high, with widths of the 95% limits of agreement ranging from 1.82 to 2.09 mm.
5. The kappa statistics indicated fair to moderate interobserver agreement, ranging from 0.39 to 0.49.
6. SLBM had a lower sensitivity (55.56% to 73.68%) compared to CIP (76.19% to 95.45%) for detecting pupils larger than 6 mm.

GAP: 
While the study provides a comparison of SLBM and CIP in terms of agreement, intraobserver and interobserver repeatability, and sensitivity for detecting larger pupils, it does not explore the impact of various environmental factors (such as ambient lighting

abstract: To evaluate prepuce development and retractibility in a group of boys. To point out the value of circumcision and prepucial forced dilation during childhood. Prepuce development and retractibility were evaluated in 400 boys ages between 0-16 year old. In boys under 1 year prepuce retractibility (assessed only in children who did not undergo forced dilation previously) was type I (non retractile) in 71.5% whereas type V (completely retractile) was only 5.5%. In adolescent boys type I prepuce was observed in 1 boy only, 1.6%, whereas type V was observed in 82.3%. Furthermore, it was observed that at the time of examination for the study 106 boys who had undergone forced dilation at an earlier age had balano-prepucial adhesions again, which demonstrates that prepuce adheres again to glans penis in many boys after a forced dilation is performed. Only 11 boys were considered in need for circumcision, three of them for prepucial orifice stenosis, which prevented normal micturition, causing a prepucial sac, one case due to a constrictive ring below the prepucial edge that would have prevented ulterior retractability, two cases with repetitive balanopostitis, and five cases secondary to xerosol balanitis, accounting for 2.7% of all examined boys.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that forced dilation in early childhood may lead to a higher recurrence rate of adhesions and reduced preputial retractility in later years compared to natural development without forced intervention.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [Key findings include the high prevalence of non-retractile prepuce in young boys, the increase in retractility in adolescent boys, the recurrence of adhesions after forced dilation, and the low incidence of circumcision needed.]

GAP: [The knowledge gap lies in understanding the long-term effects of forced dilation on prepuce retractility and the potential prevention or reduction of adhesions through alternative methods other than forced dilation.]

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that forced dilation in early childhood may lead to a higher recurrence rate of adhesions and reduced preputial retractility in later years compared to natural development without forced intervention.

abstract: Medical records of 220 patients hospitalized for acute diverticulitis between June 1, 2002 and September 1, 2009 were reviewed. Acute diverticulitis was diagnosed by clinical criteria and characteristic CT findings. Fifteen patients were excluded either because of questionable CT or hematochezia. Mean age was 61.8±14.3 years (61% females). Clinical parameters, laboratory results, imaging, endoscopic and histopathological reports, and long-term patients' outcome were analyzed. One hundred patients (aged 61.8±13.3 y, 54.1% females), underwent an early (4 to 6 wk) colonoscopy after hospital discharge. There were no significant differences in patients' characteristics or survival between those with or without colonoscopy (4±1.9 vs. 4.2±2.1 y, P=0.62). No colonic malignancy was detected. However, in 32 patients (32%) at least 1 polyp was found. Only 1 was determined as an advanced adenoma. No new or different diagnosis was made after colonoscopy.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the presence of non-advanced polyps in patients with acute diverticulitis, even in the absence of colorectal cancer, may be associated with increased long-term risk of developing colorectal cancer compared to patients without such polyps.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study reviewed medical records of 220 patients hospitalized for acute diverticulitis, with 100 patients undergoing an early colonoscopy after hospital discharge. No significant differences in patient characteristics or survival were found between those who underwent colonoscopy and those who did not. Polyps were found in 32% of the patients, with only one being classified as an advanced adenoma.

GAP: The study did not explore whether the presence of polyps, particularly non-advanced adenomas, might have implications for the long-term health outcomes of patients with acute diverticulitis beyond the detection of colon cancer.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the presence of non-advanced polyps in patients with acute diverticulitis, even in the absence of colorectal cancer, may be associated with increased long-term risk of developing colorectal cancer compared to patients without such polyps.

abstract: To investigate whether problems in instrumental activities of daily living (IADL) can add to conventionally used clinical measurements in helping to predict a diagnosis of dementia at 1- and 2-year follow-up. Multicenter prospective cohort study. Memory clinics in Europe. Individuals aged 55 and older without dementia. IADLs were measured using pooled activities from five informant-based questionnaires. Structural equation modeling (SEM) was used to investigate the relation between IADLs and dementia. Age, sex, education, depression, and cognitive measures (Mini-Mental State Examination and verbal memory) were included in the model. Five hundred thirty-one participants had baseline and 1-year follow-up assessments; 69 (13.0%) of these had developed dementia at 1-year follow-up. At 2-year follow-up, 481 participants were seen, of whom 100 (20.8%) had developed dementia. Participants with IADL disabilities at baseline had a higher conversion rate (24.4%) than participants without IADL disabilities (16.7%) (chi-square = 4.28, degrees of freedom = 1, P = .04). SEM showed that IADL disability could help predict dementia in addition to the measured variables at 1-year follow-up (odds ratio (OR) = 2.20, 95% confidence interval (CI) = 1.51-3.13) and 2-year follow-up (OR = 2.11, 95% CI = 1.33-3.33).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that impaired performance in IADLs is associated with increased neurodegenerative changes in brain regions responsible for executive function and motor coordination, as measured by neuroimaging techniques, which in turn contribute to the development of dementia.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that individuals with IADL disabilities at baseline had a higher conversion rate to dementia compared to those without IADL disabilities. Additionally, structural equation modeling (SEM) indicated that IADL disability could help predict dementia beyond the conventional clinical measurements such as age, sex, education, depression, and cognitive measures at both 1-year and 2-year follow-ups.

GAP: While the study demonstrates the predictive power of IADL disabilities for dementia, it does not explore the specific mechanisms through which IADL impairments contribute to the development or progression of dementia.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that impaired performance in IADLs is associated with increased neurodegenerative changes in brain regions responsible for executive function and motor coordination, as measured by neuroimaging techniques, which in turn contribute to the development of dementia.

abstract: The relationship between the use of an endoscope during ventriculoperitoneal shunt (VPS) procedures and infection remains poorly defined. In this study, we sought to analyze whether the simultaneous use of an endoscope could in fact increase the infection rate associated with VPS procedures. This study included 438 VPS procedures, 49 in which an endoscope was used (11.2%) and 389 in which an endoscope was not used (88.8%). The infection rates in these 2 main groups were calculated and compared. Subsequently, 4 new groups were created, composed of patients with a shunt inserted for the first time (groups 1A and 1B) and patients with a shunt reviewed or inserted for a second time (groups 2A and 2B). Groups 1A and 2A comprised patients in whom an endoscope was used simultaneously with VPS surgery, and groups 1B and 2B comprised patients in whom an endoscope was not used. These groups were compared to determine the infection rate. The overall infection rate was 18.5%, including 22.4% in the groups in which an endoscope was used and 18% in those in which an endoscope was not used (P = 0.449). Groups 1A and 1B and groups 2A and 2B were matched for possible intervening risk factors. The infection rate was 28.6% in group 1A and 16.2% in group 1B (P = 0.27), and 20% in group 2A and 19.8% in group 2B (P = 0.977).
hypothesis: HYP
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [The study analyzed the relationship between using an endoscope during ventriculoperitoneal shunt (VPS) procedures and the infection rate. The overall infection rate was 18.5%, with higher rates observed when an endoscope was used (22.4%) compared to when it was not used (18%), although this difference was not statistically significant (P = 0.449). When comparing groups based on whether it was the first or second time a shunt was inserted, the infection rate was significantly higher in the group where an endoscope was used for the first insertion (28.6%) compared to where it was not used (16.2%) (P = 0.27), but no significant difference was found for subsequent insertions (P = 0.977).]

GAP: [While the study did not find a statistically significant difference in infection rates overall or for subsequent shunt insertions, there was a trend towards increased infections when an endoscope was used for the first shunt insertion. However, the study did not explore potential mechanisms or additional variables that might explain why the use of an endoscope could be more risky for the first-time procedure.]

HYP

abstract: To compare children's, parents' and physicians' perceptions of children's body size. We administered a structured questionnaire of body size perception using a descriptive Likert scale keyed to body image figures to children ages 12 to 18 years. The same scale was given to parents of children ages 5 to 18 years. The sample consisted of 91 children and their parents being seen in the Pediatric Gastroenterology Clinic for concerns unrelated to overweight. Weight and height of the children were measured, and body mass index (BMI) was calculated. The children's BMI percentiles were categorized as underweight (<15th), normal (15th-85th), overweight (85th-95th), and obese (95th and above). The attending physician independently completed the body image and description scale and indicated the figure that most accurately represented the patient without reference to BMI standards. Accuracy of the patients', parents', and doctors' estimates were statistically compared. The sample population consisted of 6.4% underweight, 70.5% normal weight, 7.7% overweight, and 15.4% obese. Forty-four percent of parents underestimated children's body size using word descriptions and 47% underestimated using figures. Forty percent of the children underestimated their own body size using descriptions and 43% underestimated using figures. The physicians in this study had a higher percentage of correct estimates; however, they underestimated 33% of the patients using both word descriptions and figures. Some obese children were not recognized, and several average children were perceived as underweight.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that there will be a significant correlation between the parents' level of education and their accuracy in estimating their child's body size, with more educated parents having a higher accuracy rate in estimating their child's body size.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [The study found that there is a significant discrepancy between children's, parents', and physicians' perceptions of children's body sizes. Parents and children often underestimate their body size, while physicians have a higher accuracy but still misclassify some patients, particularly those who are obese or average in size.]

GAP: [The study does not explore whether the level of parental education or socioeconomic status affects the accuracy of body size perception among parents.]

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that there will be a significant correlation between the parents' level of education and their accuracy in estimating their child's body size, with more educated parents having a higher accuracy rate in estimating their child's body size.

abstract: In a prospective study 218 preschool children were enrolled (stratified in 2 training programs, one specialized for phonologic awareness in order to prevent dyslexia, the other consisting in training of general perception) during the last year of kindergarten. After finishing the first grade 131 children were compared in their reading and writing abilities. In the whole group only a slight difference was found between both training modalities concerning their writing abilities. However, children with a history of hearing loss, actual hearing loss or pathologic middle ear findings profited most from the specialized training program compared to the control in their reading abilities.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that children with a history of hearing loss who received specialized phonologic awareness training will demonstrate sustained improvements in reading abilities and show altered brain activity patterns indicative of enhanced phonological processing when assessed six months post-training compared to those who received general perception training.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that there was only a slight difference in writing abilities between children who participated in a specialized phonologic awareness training program and those who participated in a general perception training program. However, children with a history of hearing loss, actual hearing loss, or pathologic middle ear findings showed greater improvement in reading abilities after participating in the specialized phonologic awareness training program compared to the control group.

GAP: The study did not explore whether the observed benefits in reading abilities for children with hearing issues persist over time or if these improvements can be linked to specific changes in neural processing or cognitive function.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that children with a history of hearing loss who received specialized phonologic awareness training will demonstrate sustained improvements in reading abilities and show altered brain activity patterns indicative of enhanced phonological processing when assessed six months post-training compared to those who received general perception training.

abstract: Multiple sclerosis (MS) is an immune-mediated inflammatory demyelinating disease of uncertain etiology. Although the mechanisms of inducting autoimmunity by some of the infectious agents have been investigated, there is not yet enough research on streptococcal infections.MATERIAL/ To understand the effect of past group A streptococcal infection on MS, antistreptolysin O (ASO) and antideoxyribonuclease B (ADNase B) were measured in 21 patients with relapsing-remitting MS and 21 healthy blood donors by nephelometric assay. ADNase B levels in the patients with MS were found to be significantly higher than in the controls (p<0.001); however, ASO levels were similar in both groups.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that increased ADNase B levels in patients with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis are indicative of a heightened immune response to group A streptococcal infection, which may contribute to the pathogenesis of MS through the activation of pro-inflammatory pathways.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that patients with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis had significantly higher levels of ADNase B compared to healthy controls, while ASO levels did not differ between the two groups. This suggests a possible association between group A streptococcal infection and MS through ADNase B.

GAP: Despite the observed difference in ADNase B levels, the study did not investigate whether ADNase B itself might play a direct role in the pathogenesis of MS or if it serves as a biomarker for previous streptococcal infection contributing to MS development.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that increased ADNase B levels in patients with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis are indicative of a heightened immune response to group A streptococcal infection, which may contribute to the pathogenesis of MS through the activation of pro-inflammatory pathways.

abstract: The endogenous estradiol metabolite, 2-methoxyestradiol (2ME), has been shown to be a potent inhibitor of cell growth and a strong anti-angiogenic substance. We investigated for the first time whether in vitro combinations of 2ME with various chemotherapeutic compounds may result in an additive inhibitory effect on the proliferation of human ovary cancer cells. As a model two different human ovary cancer cell lines were used. All cell lines were incubated with equimolar concentrations of 2ME (0.8-25 microM) and the chemotherapeutics epirubicine, doxorubicine, paclitaxel, docetaxel, carboplatin, vinorelbine, 5-fluorouracil and mafosfamide. Proliferation was measured after four days using the ATP-chemosensitivity test. For both ovary cancer cell lines a significant additive effect of 2ME with epirubicine and carboplatin was observed at the lower concentration range of these chemotherapeutic substances.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that 2ME will exhibit additive inhibitory effects when combined with paclitaxel and docetaxel at lower concentrations on the proliferation of human ovary cancer cells, similar to its effects observed with epirubicine and carboplatin.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study demonstrated that 2-methoxyestradiol (2ME) showed significant additive effects when combined with epirubicine and carboplatin at lower concentrations on the proliferation of human ovary cancer cells.

GAP: The study did not explore the potential additive effects of 2ME with other chemotherapeutic agents or investigate the mechanism behind the observed additive effects.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that 2ME will exhibit additive inhibitory effects when combined with paclitaxel and docetaxel at lower concentrations on the proliferation of human ovary cancer cells, similar to its effects observed with epirubicine and carboplatin.

abstract: Reconstructing the natural joint line in knee revision surgery improves clinical and functional outcome but may be challenging when both cartilage and bone were removed during previous operations. Assessing joint lines (JLs) by means of bony landmarks is inadvisable because of large variations in human anatomy. Because of the inherent symmetry of the human body, we hypothesised that JLs may be directly assessed by measuring the distances from the bony landmarks to the JL of the contralateral knee by means of radiographic images. Using scaled weight-bearing radiographs in anteroposterior view of both knees, two independent observers measured the distances from the fibular head, the medial and lateral epicondyle, and the adductor tubercle to the JL. A two-sided p value of ≤0.05 was considered statistically significant. Two hundred knees of 100 patients (50 men and 50 women) were examined. For the fibular head, the mean difference between the treated and the control knee was 0.0 mm with narrow confidence limits ranging from -1.1 to 1.1.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the method of measuring joint line distances from bony landmarks to the contralateral knee will yield consistent results for the medial and lateral epicondyles and adductor tubercle in a diverse cohort of patients undergoing knee revision surgery, regardless of the extent of previous cartilage and bone removal.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that reconstructing the natural joint line in knee revision surgery improves clinical and functional outcomes, but assessing joint lines using bony landmarks can be unreliable due to anatomical variations. Radiographic measurements from the contralateral knee provided consistent results for the fibular head, with a mean difference of 0.0 mm between the treated and control knees.

GAP: While the study used the fibular head for comparison and found consistent results, it did not explore other bony landmarks or different methods for assessing joint lines. Additionally, the study did not provide information on the reliability and accuracy of the method across different types of knee revisions or patient populations.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the method of measuring joint line distances from bony landmarks to the contralateral knee will yield consistent results for the medial and lateral epicondyles and adductor tubercle in a diverse cohort of patients undergoing knee revision surgery, regardless of the extent of previous cartilage and bone removal.

abstract: There are a number of factors responsible for the longevity of unicompartmental knee replacements (UKR). These include the magnitude of postoperative alignment and the type of material used. The effect of component design and material on postoperative alignment, however, has not been explored. We retrospectively reviewed 89 patients who underwent UKR with robotic guidance. Patients were divided into two groups, according to whether they had received an all-polyethylene inlay component (Inlay group) or a metal-backed onlay component (Onlay group). We explored the magnitude of mechanical alignment correction obtained in both groups. Mean postoperative mechanical alignment was significantly closer to neutral in the Onlay group (mean=2.8°; 95% CI=2.4°, 3.2°) compared to the Inlay group (mean=3.9°; 95% CI=3.4°, 4.4°) (R2=0.65; P=0.003), adjusting for gender, BMI, age, side and preoperative mechanical alignment (Fig. 2). Further exploration revealed that the thickness of the tibial polyethyelene insert had a significant effect on postoperative alignment when added to the model (R2=0.68; P=0.01).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the superior postoperative alignment achieved with the metal-backed onlay component will correlate with improved long-term clinical outcomes, such as reduced knee pain and enhanced functional ability, compared to the all-polyethylene inlay component.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that mechanical alignment correction was significantly better in the Onlay group compared to the Inlay group after unicompartmental knee replacement surgery. Additionally, the thickness of the tibial polyethylene insert had a significant effect on postoperative alignment.

GAP: While the study provides insights into the impact of component design and material on postoperative alignment, it does not explore how these factors might influence the longevity and success of the surgical outcome beyond immediate alignment correction.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the superior postoperative alignment achieved with the metal-backed onlay component will correlate with improved long-term clinical outcomes, such as reduced knee pain and enhanced functional ability, compared to the all-polyethylene inlay component.

abstract: To determine whether there is a relationship between VEGF expression and renal vein and vena cava invasion in stage pT3 renal cell carcinoma and to evaluate the impact of VEGF expression on survival in pT3 renal cell carcinoma. 78 patients with a pT3a or pT3b tumour without vena cava invasion or pT3b tumour with vena cava invasion were compared for age, gender, Fuhrman grade and immunohistochemical expression of VEGF. All these variables were submitted to univariate and multivariate analysis to establish their impact on survival. Only tumour size appeared to be significantly different between the 3 groups. On univariate analysis, invasion of the perirenal fat, lymph node involvement, distant metastases and VEGF expression were significantly associated with survival (p<0.01). On multivariate analysis, lymph node involvement, distant metastases and VEGF expression (OR 6.07) were identified as independent predictive factors of survival.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that among patients with pT3 renal cell carcinoma, VEGF expression will have a stronger impact on survival in those with high-grade tumors compared to those with low-grade tumors.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that VEGF expression was significantly associated with survival in patients with stage pT3 renal cell carcinoma. Additionally, lymph node involvement, distant metastases, and VEGF expression were identified as independent predictive factors of survival on multivariate analysis. Tumour size was the only variable that differed significantly between groups.

GAP: While the study identifies VEGF expression as an independent predictor of survival, it does not explore the mechanism by which VEGF expression influences patient outcomes or whether the effect of VEGF expression on survival differs between subgroups of patients based on other clinical characteristics.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that among patients with pT3 renal cell carcinoma, VEGF expression will have a stronger impact on survival in those with high-grade tumors compared to those with low-grade tumors.

abstract: Deaths from injury and poisoning (suicide, accidents, undetermined deaths, and homicide) are the major cause of death among young men aged 15-39 years in England and Wales and have been increasing in recent years.AIM: To describe common characteristics among young men who die from injury and poisoning. We employed a retrospective survey methodology to investigate factors associated with deaths by injury and poisoning among young men aged 15-39 years (n = 268) in Merseyside and Cheshire during 1995. Data were collected from Coroner's inquest notes and General Practitioner records. The most common cause of death was poisoning by alcohol and drugs (29.1%, n = 78). A high proportion of cases were unemployed (39.4%, n = 106). Cases were also more likely to be single compared to the general population (74.2% vs 55.5%). Self-destructive behaviour was evident in 77% of deaths (n = 206).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that among young men aged 15-39 years who die from poisoning by alcohol and drugs, those who are both unemployed and single are at significantly higher risk compared to those who are either employed or married, due to increased isolation and reduced access to support services.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include an increase in deaths from injury and poisoning among young men aged 15-39 years in England and Wales, with poisoning by alcohol and drugs being the most common cause. A significant number of these deaths involved individuals who were unemployed and single, and there was a strong association with self-destructive behavior.

GAP: While the study identifies several risk factors such as unemployment and single status, it does not explore the potential interaction between these factors and the use of alcohol and drugs in contributing to these deaths. Additionally, the study does not delve into the role of mental health issues or access to support services in these deaths.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that among young men aged 15-39 years who die from poisoning by alcohol and drugs, those who are both unemployed and single are at significantly higher risk compared to those who are either employed or married, due to increased isolation and reduced access to support services.

abstract: To determine the duration of continuing pregnancy after antenatal corticosteroid (AC) administration and to evaluate the potential opportunity for rescue AC. Retrospective analysis of women at 24-32 weeks' gestation who received AC at one institution. Six hundred ninety-two women received AC. Two hundred forty-seven (35.7%) delivered at>or = 34 weeks' gestation. Three hundred twenty-one (46.4%) delivered within 1 week of AC; 92 of those women (13.3%) delivered within 24 hours. Only 124 (17.9%) remained pregnant 1 week after AC and delivered at<34 weeks. The latter were compared to women delivering>2 week after AC but>or = 34 weeks. More likely to deliver at<34 weeks were those women who received AC for premature preterm rupture of membranes (OR 3.83, 95% CI 2.06-7.17), twins (OR 2.90, 95% CI 1.42-5.95) or before 28 weeks (OR 2.21, 95% CI 1.38-3.52).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that women who receive a second dose of antenatal corticosteroids within 24 hours of their initial dose will have a significantly higher probability of remaining pregnant for at least one additional week without delivering at less than 34 weeks compared to those who do not receive a second dose.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that among women receiving antenatal corticosteroids (AC) between 24-32 weeks' gestation, only 17.9% remained pregnant for a week without delivering at less than 34 weeks. The odds of delivering less than 34 weeks were higher for women who received AC for premature preterm rupture of membranes, were carrying twins, or had received AC before 28 weeks.

GAP: The study did not explore whether there is a difference in the effectiveness of AC based on the timing of administration relative to delivery outcomes beyond 34 weeks, nor did it investigate whether a second dose of AC could improve pregnancy continuation.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that women who receive a second dose of antenatal corticosteroids within 24 hours of their initial dose will have a significantly higher probability of remaining pregnant for at least one additional week without delivering at less than 34 weeks compared to those who do not receive a second dose.

abstract: The pressures delivered by autotitrating continuous positive airways pressure (CPAP) devices not only treat obstructive sleep apnoea (OSA) effectively but also give potentially interesting physiological information about the forces impinging on the pharynx. In earlier work from this unit, we used correlations between autoCPAP pressure and both OSA severity and obesity, to construct an algorithm to estimate the fixed CPAP pressure a patient required for subsequent clinical use. We wished to discover if these relationships could be reliably extended to a much more obese group. We performed a prospective cohort study in an obese population. Measurements of obesity were made, OSA severity was recorded, and the 95th centile autoCPAP pressure was recorded during 1 week of autoCPAP. Spearman's rank correlation was performed between measurements of obesity and autoCPAP pressure, and between OSA severity and autoCPAP pressure. Fifty-four obese individuals (median body mass index (BMI) 43.0 kg/m(2)), 52 % of whom had OSA (apnoea-hypopnoea index (AHI) ≥ 15), had a median 95th centile autoCPAP pressure of 11.8 cmH2O. We found no significant correlation between autoCPAP pressure and neck circumference, waist circumference or BMI. There was a moderate correlation between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity (AHI r = 0.34, p = 0.02; oxygen desaturation index (ODI) r = 0.48, p < 0.001).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the relationship between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity, as indicated by AHI and ODI, will differ significantly among subgroups of obese individuals with OSA based on their comorbidities or specific types of OSA.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that there was no significant correlation between autoCPAP pressure and measures of obesity such as neck circumference, waist circumference, or BMI. However, there was a moderate correlation between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity, measured by AHI and ODI.

GAP: Despite the moderate correlation between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity, the study did not explore whether the relationship between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity holds true across different subgroups of patients, such as those with varying levels of comorbidities or specific types of OSA.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the relationship between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity, as indicated by AHI and ODI, will differ significantly among subgroups of obese individuals with OSA based on their comorbidities or specific types of OSA.

abstract: The objective of the study was to determine whether risk of recurrent preterm birth differs based on the clinical presentation of a prior spontaneous preterm birth (SPTB): advanced cervical dilatation (ACD), preterm premature rupture of membranes (PPROM), or preterm labor (PTL). This retrospective cohort study included singleton pregnancies from 2009 to 2014 complicated by a history of prior SPTB. Women were categorized based on the clinical presentation of their prior preterm delivery as having ACD, PPROM, or PTL. Risks for sonographic short cervical length and recurrent SPTB were compared between women based on the clinical presentation of their prior preterm birth. Log-linear regression was used to control for confounders. Of 522 patients included in this study, 96 (18.4%) had prior ACD, 246 (47.1%) had prior PPROM, and 180 (34.5%) had prior PTL. Recurrent PTB occurred in 55.2% of patients with a history of ACD compared with 27.2% of those with PPROM and 32.2% with PTL (P = .001). The mean gestational age at delivery was significantly lower for those with a history of ACD (34.0 weeks) compared with women with prior PPROM (37.2 weeks) or PTL (37.0 weeks) (P = .001). The lowest mean cervical length prior to 24 weeks was significantly shorter in patients with a history of advanced cervical dilation when compared with the other clinical presentations.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that women with a history of SPTB characterized by advanced cervical dilatation will exhibit distinct patterns of uterine and cervical gene expression compared to women with SPTB characterized by preterm premature rupture of membranes or preterm labor, which may explain the higher risk of recurrent preterm birth and shorter cervical length observed in the former group.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that women who experienced a prior spontaneous preterm birth (SPTB) with advanced cervical dilatation (ACD) had a higher risk of recurrent preterm birth (PTB) and delivered at a significantly lower gestational age compared to those with preterm premature rupture of membranes (PPROM) or preterm labor (PTL). Additionally, women with a history of ACD had a shorter cervical length prior to 24 weeks of gestation.

GAP: The study did not explore whether the differences in recurrence rates and cervical length could be attributed to specific molecular or genetic factors associated with the different clinical presentations of SPTB.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that women with a history of SPTB characterized by advanced cervical dilatation will exhibit distinct patterns of uterine and cervical gene expression compared to women with SPTB characterized by preterm premature rupture of membranes or preterm labor, which may explain the higher risk of recurrent preterm birth and shorter cervical length observed in the former group.

abstract: It is unclear whether traveling long distances to high-volume centers would compensate for travel burden among patients undergoing rectal cancer resection. The purpose of this study was to determine whether operative volume outweighs the advantages of being treated locally by comparing the outcomes of patients with rectal cancer treated at local, low-volume centers versus far, high-volume centers. This was a population-based study. The National Cancer Database was queried for patients with rectal cancer. Patients with stage II or III rectal cancer who underwent surgical resection between 2006 and 2012 were included. The outcomes of interest were margins, lymph node yield, receipt of neoadjuvant chemoradiation, adjuvant chemotherapy, readmission within 30 days, 30-day and 90-day mortality, and 5-year overall survival. A total of 18,605 patients met inclusion criteria; 2067 patients were in the long-distance/high-volume group and 1362 in the short-distance/low-volume group. The median travel distance was 62.6 miles for the long-distance/high-volume group and 2.3 miles for the short-distance/low-volume group. Patients who were younger, white, privately insured, and stage III were more likely to have traveled to a high-volume center. When controlled for patient factors, stage, and hospital factors, patients in the short-distance/low-volume group had lower odds of a lymph node yield ≥12 (OR = 0.51) and neoadjuvant chemoradiation (OR = 0.67) and higher 30-day (OR = 3.38) and 90-day mortality (OR = 2.07) compared with those in the long-distance/high-volume group. The short-distance/low-volume group had a 34% high risk of overall mortality at 5 years compared with the long-distance/high-volume group. We lacked data regarding patient and physician decision making and surgeon-specific factors.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that after controlling for patient and hospital characteristics, the difference in lymph node yield and neoadjuvant chemoradiation rates between short-distance, low-volume centers and long-distance, high-volume centers can be attributed to variations in surgical technique and experience of the surgeons, which may influence the quality and consistency of care provided.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that patients treated at short-distance, low-volume centers had lower odds of achieving a lymph node yield of at least 12 and receiving neoadjuvant chemoradiation, and higher rates of 30-day and 90-day mortality compared to those treated at long-distance, high-volume centers. Additionally, the 5-year overall survival was significantly higher for patients treated at high-volume centers.

GAP: The study did not explore the reasons behind the differences in outcomes, such as patient and physician decision-making processes, or the specific contributions of surgeon expertise.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that after controlling for patient and hospital characteristics, the difference in lymph node yield and neoadjuvant chemoradiation rates between short-distance, low-volume centers and long-distance, high-volume centers can be attributed to variations in surgical technique and experience of the surgeons, which may influence the quality and consistency of care provided.

abstract: It is commonly accepted that pathological gambling results from the interaction of multiple risk factors. Among these, dopamine replacement therapy (DRT) prescribed for Parkinson disease can be cited. Another dopamine agonist, aripiprazole, could be a new risk factor. We decided to explore this potential adverse drug reaction (ADR). Based on a cohort of 166 pathological gamblers starting treatment in our department, data of each of the 8 patients treated by aripiprazole at inclusion were analyzed. The patients involved were schizophrenic or bipolar, mostly young men with a history of addictive disorders and regular gambling prior to the prescription of aripiprazole. For each one of them, the causality of aripiprazole was considered, using an algorithm. The probability that pathological gambling is actually due to aripiprazole is "possible" in 7 cases out of 8, and "doubtful" in one.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that aripiprazole modulates dopamine receptor D2 signaling, leading to increased impulsivity and risk-taking behaviors, which may contribute to the development of pathological gambling in individuals with pre-existing addictive disorders.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that among 8 patients treated with aripiprazole for schizophrenia or bipolar disorder, the probability that pathological gambling is due to aripiprazole is "possible" in 7 cases and "doubtful" in one. The patients were mostly young men with a history of addictive disorders.

GAP: The study does not provide direct evidence for causation but only assesses the likelihood of a causal relationship using an algorithm. Additionally, it does not explore the underlying mechanisms through which aripiprazole might influence gambling behavior.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that aripiprazole modulates dopamine receptor D2 signaling, leading to increased impulsivity and risk-taking behaviors, which may contribute to the development of pathological gambling in individuals with pre-existing addictive disorders.

abstract: Using murine models, we have shown that the lysosomotropic amine, chloroquine, is effective in the prevention of graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) mediated by donor T cells reactive with recipient minor histocompatibility antigens (MiHCs). Because lysosomotropic amines can suppress major histocompatibility complex (MHC) class II antigen presentation, their mechanism of action is potentially different from current immune suppressant drugs used to control GVHD such as cyclosporine. We investigated the use of cyclosporine and the lysosomotropic amines chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine in combination for additive or synergistic immunosuppression on T-cell responses in vitro to MiHC and MHC in mice. We found that similar concentrations of chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine suppress the T-cell response to MiHC in mice (C57BL/6 anti-BALB.B) and that lysosomotropic amines in combination with cyclosporine result in synergistic suppression of a proliferative response to MiHC. Similar suppression and synergy appear to be present in an alloreactive response (C57BL/6 anti-BALB/c). Direct inhibition by chloroquine of T-cell proliferative responses induced by anti-CD3epsilon in the absence of antigen-presenting cells is present at higher concentrations than that required to suppress responses to MiHC or MHC. Chloroquine appears to induce decreased T-cell viability at high concentrations. This effect does not appear to be due to decreased T-cell production of interleukin-2 or interferon-gamma. At lower concentrations (<25 microg/ml), chloroquine can also decrease the ability of antigen-presenting cells to stimulate an a C57BL/6 anti-BALB/c T-cell response and can inhibit MHC class II expression after activation with lipopolysaccharide.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine exert their immunosuppressive effects through multiple mechanisms, including direct inhibition of T-cell proliferation at higher concentrations and modulation of antigen-presenting cell function at lower concentrations, which may involve alterations in lysosomal function and/or changes in the expression of co-stimulatory molecules.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study shows that chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine are effective in suppressing T-cell responses to minor histocompatibility antigens (MiHCs) and major histocompatibility complex (MHC) antigens when used alone or in combination with cyclosporine. These lysosomotropic amines can directly inhibit T-cell proliferation and antigen-presenting cell function, leading to synergistic immunosuppression.

GAP: While the study demonstrates the effectiveness of chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine in suppressing T-cell responses and their potential synergy with cyclosporine, it does not explore the exact mechanisms by which these amines affect T-cell viability and antigen-presenting cell function at different concentrations.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine exert their immunosuppressive effects through multiple mechanisms, including direct inhibition of T-cell proliferation at higher concentrations and modulation of antigen-presenting cell function at lower concentrations, which may involve alterations in lysosomal function and/or changes in the expression of co-stimulatory molecules.

abstract: To assess the outcomes of patients with nasopharyngeal carcinoma (NPC) whose treatment was determined by computerized tomography (CT) and/or magnetic resonance imaging staging and to analyze the impact of induction chemotherapy and accelerated fractionated radiotherapy. The analysis is based on 122 of 143 previously untreated patients with NPC treated with radiation therapy at The University of Texas M. D. Anderson Cancer Center between 1983 and 1992. Excluded were 4 patients treated with palliative intent, 4 children, 12 patients not staged with CT, and 1 patient who died of a cerebrovascular accident prior to completion of treatment. The stage distribution was as follows: AJCC Stage I-2, Stage II-7, Stage III-12, Stage IV-101; Tl-15, T2-33, T3-22, T4-52; N0-32, N1-10, N2-47, N3-32, Nx-1. Fifty-nine (48%) patients had squamous cell carcinoma; 63 (52%) had lymphoepitheliomas, undifferentiated NPC or poorly differentiated carcinoma, NOS (UNPC). Sixty-seven patients (65 with Stage IV disease) received induction chemotherapy. Fifty-eight patients (24 of whom had induction chemotherapy) were treated with the concomitant boost fractionation schedule. The median follow-up for surviving patients was 57 months. The overall actuarial 2- and 5-year survival rates were 78 and 68%, respectively. Forty-nine patients (40%) had disease recurrence. Thirty-three (27%) had local regional failures; 19 at the primary site only, 8 in the neck and 6 in both. Local failure occurred in 31% of patients staged T4 compared to 13% of T1-T3 (p = 0.007). Sixteen patients failed at distant sites alone. Among Stage IV patients the 5-year actuarial rates for patients who did and did not receive induction chemotherapy were as follows: overall survival: 68 vs. 56% (p = 0.02), freedom from relapse: 64 vs. 37% (p = 0.01), and local control: 86 vs. 56% (p = 0.009). The actuarial 5-year distant failure rate in patients with UNPC who were treated with induction chemotherapy and controlled in the primary and neck was 13%. In patients who did not receive chemotherapy, the actuarial 5-year local control rates for patients treated with concomitant boost or conventional fractionation were 66 and 67%, respectively.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that induction chemotherapy enhances local control and reduces the risk of local failure in nasopharyngeal carcinoma patients with T4 stage disease compared to those with T1-T3 stage disease, and this effect may be more pronounced in patients with lymphoepitheliomas or undifferentiated NPC.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [Key findings include the overall survival rates, the impact of induction chemotherapy on local control and freedom from relapse, and the higher local failure rate in T4 stage patients compared to T1-T3 stage patients. The study also notes that among Stage IV patients, those who received induction chemotherapy had significantly better overall survival, freedom from relapse, and local control rates.]

GAP: [The study does not explore the specific mechanisms by which induction chemotherapy improves local control and freedom from relapse in NPC patients, nor does it examine whether these benefits differ across different subtypes of NPC.]

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that induction chemotherapy enhances local control and reduces the risk of local failure in nasopharyngeal carcinoma patients with T4 stage disease compared to those with T1-T3 stage disease, and this effect may be more pronounced in patients with lymphoepitheliomas or undifferentiated NPC.

abstract: Treatment of neonatal testicular torsion has two objectives: salvage of the involved testicle (which is rarely achieved) and preservation of the contralateral gonad. The second goal universally involves contralateral testicular scrotal fixation to prevent the future occurrence of contralateral torsion. However, there is controversy with regards to management of a synchronous contralateral hydrocele. It has been our policy not to address the contralateral hydrocele through an inguinal incision to minimize potential injury to the spermatic cord. Our objective in this study was to determine whether the decision to manage a contralateral hydrocele in cases of neonatal testicular torsion solely through a scrotal approach is safe and effective. We reviewed all cases of neonatal testicular torsion occurring at our institution between the years 1999 and 2006. Age at presentation, physical examination, ultrasonographic and intraoperative findings were recorded. Patients were followed after initial surgical intervention to determine the likelihood of developing a subsequent hydrocele or hernia. Thirty-seven patients were identified as presenting with neonatal torsion. Age of presentation averaged 3.5 days (range 1-14 days). Left-sided pathology was seen more commonly than the right, with a 25:12 distribution. All torsed testicles were nonviable. Twenty-two patients were noted to have a contralateral hydrocele at presentation. All hydroceles were opened through a scrotal approach at the time of contralateral scrotal fixation. No patient underwent an inguinal exploration to examine for a patent process vaginalis. None of the patients who presented with a hydrocele have developed a clinical hydrocele or hernia after an average 7.5 years (range 4.3-11.2) follow-up.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the management of contralateral hydrocele through a scrotal approach alone without inguinal exploration in neonates with testicular torsion does not increase the risk of developing a patent process vaginalis, hydrocele, or hernia within the first decade post-surgery.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study identified that in cases of neonatal testicular torsion, all patients with a contralateral hydrocele had it managed through a scrotal approach without an inguinal exploration. No patient developed a clinical hydrocele or hernia over an average follow-up period of 7.5 years.

GAP: The study does not address whether the absence of inguinal exploration during the management of a contralateral hydrocele could potentially lead to undiagnosed patent process vaginalis, which might later cause complications such as hydrocele or hernia.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the management of contralateral hydrocele through a scrotal approach alone without inguinal exploration in neonates with testicular torsion does not increase the risk of developing a patent process vaginalis, hydrocele, or hernia within the first decade post-surgery.

abstract: The purpose of this study is to measure the accuracy and reliability of normally sighted, visually impaired, and blind pedestrians at making street crossing decisions using visual and/or auditory information. Using a 5-point rating scale, safety ratings for vehicular gaps of different durations were measured along a two-lane street of one-way traffic without a traffic signal. Safety ratings were collected from 12 normally sighted, 10 visually impaired, and 10 blind subjects for eight different gap times under three sensory conditions: (1) visual plus auditory information, (2) visual information only, and (3) auditory information only. Accuracy and reliability in street crossing decision-making were calculated for each subject under each sensory condition. We found that normally sighted and visually impaired pedestrians were accurate and reliable in their street crossing decision-making ability when using either vision plus hearing or vision only (P>0.05). Under the hearing only condition, all subjects were reliable (P>0.05) but inaccurate with their street crossing decisions (P<0.05). Compared to either the normally sighted (P = 0.018) or visually impaired subjects (P = 0.019), blind subjects were the least accurate with their street crossing decisions under the hearing only condition.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that increasing the duration of auditory information will improve the accuracy of street crossing decisions for blind pedestrians, as measured by a 5-point safety rating scale, compared to shorter durations of auditory information.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that normally sighted and visually impaired pedestrians were accurate and reliable in their street crossing decision-making ability when using either vision plus hearing or vision only. However, under the auditory-only condition, all subjects were reliable but inaccurate. Blind subjects were less accurate than both normally sighted and visually impaired subjects under the auditory-only condition.

GAP: The study did not explore whether there is a threshold for auditory information that affects the accuracy of street crossing decisions for blind pedestrians. Specifically, it is unclear if increasing the duration of the auditory information might improve the accuracy of their street crossing decisions.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that increasing the duration of auditory information will improve the accuracy of street crossing decisions for blind pedestrians, as measured by a 5-point safety rating scale, compared to shorter durations of auditory information.

abstract: Sulfasalazine is a widely used anti-inflammatory agent in the treatment of inflammatory bowel disease and several rheumatological disorders. Although as many as 20% of treated patients may experience reversible, dose-dependent side effects, less frequent but potentially severe, systemic reactions have also been reported. A severe systemic reaction to sulfasalazine developed in a 21-year old female with rheumatoid arthritis characterized by eosinophilia, granulomatous enteritis and myelotoxicity, cholestatic hepatitis, and seizures. The clinical course and management of this patient are presented as well as a review of the incidence and outcome of severe systemic reactions to sulfasalazine.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that genetic polymorphisms in the cytochrome P450 enzyme family (CYP) are associated with an increased risk of developing severe systemic reactions to sulfasalazine.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: Sulfasalazine, commonly used for inflammatory bowel disease and rheumatological disorders, can cause severe systemic reactions including eosinophilia, granulomatous enteritis, myelotoxicity, cholestatic hepatitis, and seizures in some patients.

GAP: The abstract does not provide detailed information about the underlying mechanisms leading to these severe systemic reactions, nor does it explore whether genetic factors play a role in the susceptibility to such adverse events.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that genetic polymorphisms in the cytochrome P450 enzyme family (CYP) are associated with an increased risk of developing severe systemic reactions to sulfasalazine.

abstract: To determine the practices and knowledge of harmful effects regarding use of Chaalia and Pan Masala in three schools of Mahmoodabad and Chanesar Goth, Jamshed Town, Karachi, Pakistan. To achieve the objective a cross-sectional design was used in three government schools of Mahmoodabad and Chanesar Goth, Jamshed Town, Karachi. Students of either gender drawn from these schools fulfilling the inclusion and exclusion criteria were interviewed using a pre-coded structured questionnaire. Along with demographic data, questions regarding frequency of Chaalia and Pan Masala use, practices of this habit in friends and family and place of procurement of these substances, were inquired. Knowledge was assessed about harmful effects and its source of information. In addition, practices in relation to that knowledge were assessed. A total of 370 students were interviewed over a period of six weeks, of which 205 (55.4%) were boys. The ages of the students were between 10 and 15 years. Thirty one percent of the fathers and 62% of the mothers were uneducated. The frequency of use of any brand of Chaalia was found to be 94% and that of Pan Masala was 73.8%. Eighty five percent of them were regular users. A large majority (88%) procured the substances themselves from near their homes. Ninety five percent of the children had friends with the same habits. Eighty four percent were using the substances in full knowledge of their families. Chaalia was considered harmful for health by 96% and Pan Masala by 60%. Good taste was cited as a reason for continuing the habit by 88.5% of the children and use by friends by 57%. Knowledge about established harmful effects was variable. Knowledge about harmful effects was high in both "daily" and "less than daily users".
hypothesis: We hypothesize that there is a negative correlation between the frequency and duration of Chaalia and Pan Masala use among students and their level of awareness about the harmful effects, especially among those with less educated parents.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [Key findings include the high prevalence of Chaalia and Pan Masala use among schoolchildren, particularly among boys, and the significant proportion of children who are regular users. There is also a high degree of self-procurement of these substances and a strong influence of peer habits. Additionally, while a substantial portion of children are aware of the harmful effects of Chaalia and Pan Masala, there is variability in this knowledge. Finally, taste is a primary reason for continued use, alongside peer pressure.]

GAP: [The study does not explore whether there is a correlation between the level of awareness about the harmful effects of Chaalia and Pan Masala and the frequency or duration of use among students. It also does not investigate how parental education might impact the frequency of use or the child's awareness of the risks.]

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that there is a negative correlation between the frequency and duration of Chaalia and Pan Masala use among students and their level of awareness about the harmful effects, especially among those with less educated parents.

abstract: Tinnitus can be related to many different aetiologies such as hearing loss or a noise trauma, but it can also be related to the somatosensory system of the cervical spine, called cervicogenic somatic tinnitus (CST). Case studies suggest a positive effect of cervical spine treatment on tinnitus complaints in patients with CST, but no experimental studies are available. To investigate the effect of a multimodal cervical physical therapy treatment on tinnitus complaints in patients with CST. Randomized controlled trial. Patients with a combination of severe subjective tinnitus (Tinnitus Functional Index (TFI): 25-90 points) and neck complaints (Neck Bournemouth Questionnaire (NBQ) > 14 points). All patients received cervical physical therapy for 6 weeks (12 sessions). Patients were randomized in an immediate-start therapy group (n = 19) and a 6-week delayed-start therapy group (n = 19). TFI and NBQ-scores were documented at baseline, after the wait-and-see period in the delayed-start group, after treatment and after 6 weeks follow-up. The Global Perceived Effect (GPE) was documented at all measuring moments, except at baseline. In all patients (n = 38) TFI and NBQ-scores decreased significantly after treatment (p = 0.04 and p < 0.001). NBQ-scores remained significantly lower after follow-up (p = 0.001). Immediately after treatment, 53% (n = 38) experienced substantial improvement of tinnitus. This effect was maintained in 24% of patients after follow-up at six weeks.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the patients who experienced substantial improvement of tinnitus immediately after treatment will show a faster decline in TFI scores and a quicker maintenance of lower NBQ-scores compared to those who did not experience immediate improvement, as measured at various time points during and after the 6-week follow-up period.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that cervical physical therapy significantly reduced both tinnitus complaints (measured by TFI) and neck complaints (measured by NBQ) in patients with cervicogenic somatic tinnitus (CST). The improvement in tinnitus complaints was maintained up to six weeks post-treatment in 24% of the patients.

GAP: While the study demonstrates a significant reduction in tinnitus and neck complaints after cervical physical therapy, it does not provide information on the underlying mechanisms responsible for the therapeutic effect. Additionally, the study does not explore whether the duration of the therapeutic effect differs between patients who experienced immediate improvement versus those who maintained improvement over the six-week follow-up period.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the patients who experienced substantial improvement of tinnitus immediately after treatment will show a faster decline in TFI scores and a quicker maintenance of lower NBQ-scores compared to those who did not experience immediate improvement, as measured at various time points during and after the 6-week follow-up period.

abstract: The technique of induced sputum has allowed to subdivide asthma patients into inflammatory phenotypes according to their level of granulocyte airway infiltration. There are very few studies which looked at detailed sputum and blood cell counts in a large cohort of asthmatics divided into inflammatory phenotypes. The purpose of this study was to analyze sputum cell counts, blood leukocytes and systemic inflammatory markers in these phenotypes, and investigate how those groups compared with healthy subjects. We conducted a retrospective cross-sectional study on 833 asthmatics recruited from the University Asthma Clinic of Liege and compared them with 194 healthy subjects. Asthmatics were classified into inflammatory phenotypes. The total non-squamous cell count per gram of sputum was greater in mixed granulocytic and neutrophilic phenotypes as compared to eosinophilic, paucigranulocytic asthma and healthy subjects (p < 0.005). Sputum eosinophils (in absolute values and percentages) were increased in all asthma phenotypes including paucigranulocytic asthma, compared to healthy subjects (p < 0.005). Eosinophilic asthma showed higher absolute sputum neutrophil and lymphocyte counts than healthy subjects (p < 0.005), while neutrophilic asthmatics had a particularly low number of sputum macrophages and epithelial cells. All asthma phenotypes showed an increased blood leukocyte count compared to healthy subjects (p < 0.005), with paucigranulocytic asthmatics having also increased absolute blood eosinophils compared to healthy subjects (p < 0.005). Neutrophilic asthma had raised CRP and fibrinogen while eosinophilic asthma only showed raised fibrinogen compared to healthy subjects (p < 0.005).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that there is a significant correlation between elevated sputum neutrophil counts and increased levels of CRP and fibrinogen in neutrophilic asthma, leading to more severe clinical symptoms and poorer treatment response compared to other asthma phenotypes.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study identified differences in sputum and blood cell counts among various inflammatory phenotypes of asthma and compared these with healthy subjects. It found that mixed granulocytic and neutrophilic phenotypes had a higher total non-squamous cell count, and eosinophils were increased across all asthma phenotypes. Additionally, eosinophilic and neutrophilic asthmatics showed elevated levels of certain blood markers like CRP and fibrinogen.

GAP: While the study provides a detailed analysis of cellular and systemic inflammatory markers in different asthma phenotypes, it does not explore the potential correlation between specific inflammatory markers and clinical outcomes or response to treatment in these phenotypes.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that there is a significant correlation between elevated sputum neutrophil counts and increased levels of CRP and fibrinogen in neutrophilic asthma, leading to more severe clinical symptoms and poorer treatment response compared to other asthma phenotypes.

abstract: To assess the impact of the comprehensive HIV/STD Control Program established in Jamaica since the late 1980s on the HIV/AIDS epidemic. AIDS case reports, HIV testing of blood donors, antenatal clinic attenders (ANC), food service workers, sexually transmitted disease (STD) clinic attenders, female prostitutes, homosexuals and other groups were used to monitor the HIV/AIDS epidemic. Primary and secondary syphilis and cases of congenital syphilis were also monitored. National knowledge, attitude and practice (KAP) surveys were conducted in 1988, 1989, 1992, 1994 and 1996. The annual AIDS incidence rate in Jamaica increased only marginally in the past three years from 18.5 per 100000 population to 21.4 in 1997. HIV prevalence in the general population groups tested has been about 1% or less. Among those at high risk, HIV prevalence rates have risen to 6.3% (95% confidence interval 5.0-8.0) in STD clinic attenders, around 10% and 21% in female prostitutes in Kingston and Montego Bay respectively and approximately 30% among homosexuals. Syphilis rates and congenital syphilis cases have declined. The proportion of men aged 15-49 years reporting sex with a non-regular partner declined from 35% in 1994 to 26% in 1996 (P<0.001). Women ever having used condoms increased from 51% in 1988 to 62.5% in 1992 and 73% in 1994 and 1996 (P<0.001), while condom use with a non-regular partner increased from 37% in 1992 to 73% in 1996 (P= 0.006). Condom use among men was high over the period: around 81% had ever used condoms and 77% used them with non-regular partners. Gay men, inner-city adults and adolescents aged 12-14 years all reported increases in condom use while condom sales and distribution increased from around 2 million in 1985 to 10 million in 1995.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the observed increase in condom use and decrease in non-regular partnerships among high-risk populations in Jamaica can be attributed to the implementation and effectiveness of the comprehensive HIV/STD Control Program, which likely contributed to the marginal increase in AIDS incidence rates and maintained low HIV prevalence in the general
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [The study found that the HIV/AIDS epidemic in Jamaica showed only a marginal increase in AIDS incidence rates and maintained relatively low HIV prevalence in the general population (about 1%). However, among high-risk groups such as STD clinic attenders, female prostitutes, and homosexuals, HIV prevalence rates were notably higher (6.3%, 10%, and 21%-30% respectively). There was a significant decline in primary and secondary syphilis rates and congenital syphilis cases. Additionally, there were positive changes in sexual behaviors, including a decrease in non-regular partnerships and an increase in condom use among various population groups.]

GAP: [While the study provides detailed information on the prevalence and trends of HIV and syphilis, it does not explore whether these behavioral changes were directly attributable to the comprehensive HIV/STD Control Program or if they influenced the success of the program in reducing high-risk behaviors and HIV transmission.]

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the observed increase in condom use and decrease in non-regular partnerships among high-risk populations in Jamaica can be attributed to the implementation and effectiveness of the comprehensive HIV/STD Control Program, which likely contributed to the marginal increase in AIDS incidence rates and maintained low HIV prevalence in the general

abstract: The morbidity and mortality associated with Panton-Valentine leucocidin (PVL)-positive Staphylococcus aureus suggest that this toxin is a key marker of disease severity. Nevertheless, the importance of PVL in the pathogenesis of primary bacteraemia caused by S. aureus is uncertain. We have determined the prevalence of PVL-encoding genes among isolates of S. aureus from bacteraemic patients. Consecutive bacteraemia isolates of S. aureus (n=244) from patients hospitalized in 25 centres in the UK and Ireland during 2005 were screened for PVL and mecA genes. PVL-positive isolates were characterized by toxin gene profiling, PFGE, spa-typing and MIC determinations for a range of antimicrobials. Four out of 244 isolates (1.6%) were PVL-positive and susceptible to oxacillin [methicillin-susceptible S. aureus (MSSA)]. Eighty-eight out of 244 (36%) were oxacillin-resistant (methicillin-resistant S. aureus), but none was PVL-positive. The four patients (two males: 30 and 33 years; two females: 62 and 80 years) had infection foci of: skin and soft tissue, unknown, indwelling line, and surgical site, and were located at one centre in Wales, one in England and two in Ireland. One of four PVL-positive isolates was resistant to penicillin and fusidic acid, the remainder were susceptible to all antibiotics tested. Genotypic analyses showed that the four isolates represented three distinct strains; the two isolates from Ireland were related.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that Panton-Valentine leucocidin (PVL) positively correlates with increased severity and likelihood of invasive complications in patients with methicillin-susceptible primary bacteraemia caused by Staphylococcus aureus.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [The study determined the prevalence of PVL-encoding genes among S. aureus isolates from bacteraemic patients and found that only 1.6% of the isolates were PVL-positive. All PVL-positive isolates were methicillin-susceptible, while 36% of the isolates were methicillin-resistant, and none of the latter were PVL-positive. The PVL-positive isolates originated from various infection sites and locations across different countries.]

GAP: [Despite identifying the low prevalence of PVL-positive isolates and their association with MSSA status, the study does not explore whether PVL plays a role in the pathogenesis or clinical outcomes of primary bacteraemia caused by S. aureus.]

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that Panton-Valentine leucocidin (PVL) positively correlates with increased severity and likelihood of invasive complications in patients with methicillin-susceptible primary bacteraemia caused by Staphylococcus aureus.

abstract: Impaired fasting glucose (IFG) below the diagnostic threshold for diabetes mellitus (DM) is associated with macrovascular pathology and increased mortality after percutaneous coronary interventions. The study goal was to determine whether pre-operative fasting blood glucose (fB-glu) is associated with an increased mortality after coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG). During 2001-03, 1895 patients underwent primary CABG [clinical DM (CDM) in 440/1895; complete data on fB-glu for n=1375/1455]. Using pre-operative fB-glu, non-diabetics were categorized as having normal fB-glu (<5.6 mmol/L), IFG (5.6<or =fB-glu<6.1 mmol/L), or suspected DM (SDM) (>or =6.1 mmol/L). fB-glu was normal in 59%. The relative risks of 30 day and 1 year mortality compared with patients with normal fB-glu was 1.7 [95% confidence interval (CI): 0.5-5.5] and 2.9 (CI: 0.8-11.2) with IFG, 2.8 (CI: 1.1-7.2) and 1.9 (CI: 0.5-6.3) with SDM vs. 1.8 (CI: 0.8-4.0) and 1.6 (CI: 0.6-4.3) if CDM, respectively. The receiver operator characteristic area for the continuous variable fB-glu and 1 year mortality was 0.65 (P=0.002).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that among patients undergoing CABG, the increased 1-year mortality associated with impaired fasting glucose (IFG) and suspected diabetes mellitus (SDM) is more pronounced in those with coexisting hypertension compared to those without hypertension.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: Impaired fasting glucose (IFG) and suspected diabetes mellitus (SDM) are associated with increased 30-day and 1-year mortality after coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG). The risk is higher with IFG compared to SDM, but both IFG and SDM have higher risks than normal fasting blood glucose (fB-glu). The receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve analysis suggests that fB-glu can predict 1-year mortality.

GAP: While the study identifies associations between impaired fasting glucose levels and increased mortality, it does not explore whether the association holds true for different subgroups of patients or whether other metabolic parameters might modify these risks.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that among patients undergoing CABG, the increased 1-year mortality associated with impaired fasting glucose (IFG) and suspected diabetes mellitus (SDM) is more pronounced in those with coexisting hypertension compared to those without hypertension.

abstract: The influence of positron emission tomography in the management of recurrent rectal cancer is well established but its role in primary rectal cancer remains uncertain. This study therefore prospectively assesses the impact of position emission tomography scanning on the management of primary rectal cancer. Forty-six patients with advanced primary rectal cancer referred for consideration of adjuvant preoperative therapy underwent position emission tomography scanning. The referring physicians prospectively recorded each patient's stage following conventional imaging and the proposed treatment plan prior to position emission tomography scanning. This was then compared with subsequent stage and actual management implemented, and the appropriateness of position emission tomography-induced changes was noted by subsequent clinical follow-up. The surgical management of 36 of 46 patients (78 percent) was unchanged as a result of position emission tomography, even though position emission tomography upstaged disease in 3 of 36 cases (8 percent) and downstaged disease in 5 of 36 cases (14 percent). In 8 of 46 cases (17 percent), management was altered because of the position emission tomography scan findings, including 6 cases (13 percent) in which surgery was cancelled and 2 other cases (4 percent) in which the radiotherapy field was changed. Where available, follow-up confirmed the appropriateness of position emission tomography-induced management change in each case. Two patients had a change in therapy independent of the position emission tomography scan due to clinical circumstances. Overall tumor stage was changed following position emission tomography in 18 of 46 patients (39 percent).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that positron emission tomography scanning will lead to improved disease control and reduced local recurrence rates in patients with primary rectal cancer compared to those managed without PET guidance, despite initial increases in management complexity.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: [The study found that out of 46 patients with advanced primary rectal cancer, 78% had their management unchanged despite positron emission tomography (PET) scanning potentially altering their staging (upstaging or downstaging). PET scanning led to changes in management in 17% of cases, including cancellations of surgery and modifications to radiotherapy fields. Follow-up confirmed the appropriateness of these changes. Additionally, overall tumor stage was changed in 39% of patients following PET scanning.]

GAP: [The study does not provide information on how these changes in management affected patient outcomes, such as disease control, recurrence rates, or survival. There is also no data on the cost-effectiveness or long-term impact of PET scans on patient care.]

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that positron emission tomography scanning will lead to improved disease control and reduced local recurrence rates in patients with primary rectal cancer compared to those managed without PET guidance, despite initial increases in management complexity.

abstract: One of the problems with manual resuscitators is the difficulty in achieving accurate volume delivery. The volume delivered to the patient varies by the physical characteristics of the person and method. This study was designed to compare tidal volumes delivered by the squeezing method, physical characteristics and education and practice levels. 114 individuals trained in basic life support and bag-valve-mask ventilation participated in this study. Individual characteristics were obtained by the observer and the education and practice level were described by the subjects. Ventilation was delivered with a manual resuscitator connected to a microspirometer and volumes were measured. Subjects completed three procedures: one-handed, two-handed and two-handed half-compression. The mean (standard deviation) volumes for the one-handed method were 592.84 ml (SD 117.39), two-handed 644.24 ml (SD 144.7) and two-handed half-compression 458.31 ml (SD 120.91) (p<0.01).
